title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs request smuggling, response splitting, parser differentials, and downgrades from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.
Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Clte Tecl Defense
steps.instruction:
Order the Clte Tecl Defense evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Clte Tecl Defense.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Clte Tecl Defense assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Clte Tecl Defense, keep Clte Tecl Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A review protocol team handles case 7165 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Clte Tecl Defense has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Downgrade Consistency Gate · changed case · Review Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 7170 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.
